title: Short dump triage from an Outlook mail
subtitle: Outlook replay where Copilot reads a weak support mail, checks ST22, and drafts a reply.
outcome: Root cause, evidence, and a support reply drafted from SAP diagnostics.

start.text: This overnight job short dumped. Can you investigate and draft a reply?

panel.title: Outlook message
panel.body:
Subject: Overnight ABAP dump in DEV

ZARC1_DEMO_DUMP_RPT failed around 02:13. ST22 should have the dump. Please check whether this is a code issue.

dumps.summary: ARC-1 reads the recent dump list from the SAP system.

source.summary: ARC-1 reads the risky dynamic SELECT area, not the whole report.

panel.title: Risky source line
panel.code:
PARAMETERS p_table TYPE tabname.

SELECT * FROM (p_table) INTO TABLE @DATA(result).

options.label: Draft Outlook reply
options.description: Write a clear reply with root cause and next action.

options.label: Show defensive patch
options.description: Show the simulated validation diff that would prevent this dump.

reply.text:
Here's a draft reply for operations:

## Root cause

`ZARC1_DEMO_DUMP_RPT` hit a `DBSQL_SQL_ERROR` at 02:13. The report takes a dynamic table name and runs `SELECT * FROM (p_table)` without validating it, so an invalid `p_table` dumps the job. This is a **code issue**, not bad data.

## Recommended fix

- Validate `p_table` against DDIC metadata (`DD02L`) before the SELECT.
- Return a controlled error for unknown tables instead of dumping.

I can prepare the defensive patch for a developer to review.

panel.title: Defensive validation diff
panel.code:
+SELECT SINGLE tabname FROM dd02l
+  WHERE tabname = @p_table AND as4local = @space
+  INTO @DATA(valid_table).
+IF sy-subrc <> 0.
+  MESSAGE e001(zarc1) WITH p_table.
+ENDIF.
 SELECT * FROM (p_table) INTO TABLE @DATA(result).

done.text: Support didn't need ADT open to get the first real SAP evidence — but the fix still stays a human-reviewed development task.
